overview.tagline: A classic Bergen County suburb with walkable charm, excellent schools, and the Oradell Reservoir
overview.description: Oradell is a charming residential borough in central Bergen County known for its tree-lined streets, walkable downtown, excellent schools, and the scenic Oradell Reservoir. With a population of about 8,300, the borough offers a quintessential Bergen County suburban experience. Oradell is home to the Oradell Reservoir, a major water supply for the region, and its surrounding open space provides a natural centerpiece for the community. The borough features a pleasant downtown along Kinderkamack Road with local shops and restaurants, and its schools are well-regarded within the River Dell Regional district.
overview.schoolDistrict: Oradell Public Schools / River Dell Regional
highlights: Scenic Oradell Reservoir and surrounding green space
Excellent schools in the River Dell Regional district
Tree-lined residential streets with classic suburban homes
NJ Transit Pascack Valley Line station for NYC commuters

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Bergen County conducts periodic revaluations. Oradell's walkability and school district quality are key drivers of property values.
realEstate.recentTrends: Oradell's market has been strong, with consistent demand from families attracted to the walkable downtown, school quality, and classic suburban character. Homes near the reservoir and downtown command premium prices. The market favors sellers with limited inventory.
neighborhoodNotes: Kinderkamack Road corridor — Walking distance to shops, restaurants, and the train station
Oradell Avenue area — Classic colonial homes on tree-lined streets
Reservoir section — Properties near the Oradell Reservoir with scenic views
Grant Avenue/Elm Street — Family-friendly streets near schools
New Milford border area — Diverse housing options near Hackensack River

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection provided by a contracted hauler for the Borough.
details: Trash pickup occurs twice per week
Containers at curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Maximum 50 lbs per container with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by scheduling with DPW
Holiday schedule posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling collected on a regular schedule.
details: Commingled recyclables collected biweekly
Paper and cardboard on alternate weeks
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through November
Recycling bins provided by the Borough
Special recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Ridgewood Water, which operates the Oradell Reservoir. Sewer service through the Borough and Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Ridgewood Water provides water supply from the Oradell Reservoir system
Sewer service through Bergen County Utilities Authority (BCUA)
Water billing handled by Ridgewood Water
Annual water quality reports available from Ridgewood Water
Emergency water issues handled 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Permits required for construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning Board of Adjustment meets monthly
Planning Board reviews site plans and subdivisions
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of occupancy required for new construction and major renovations

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Borough manages commuter parking at the train station and downtown street parking.
details: Commuter parking permits available at the Oradell train station
Waitlist may apply for station parking
Street parking in downtown subject to posted time limits
Overnight parking restrictions on borough streets
Snow emergency parking rules enforced

additionalServices.title: Oradell Free Public Library
additionalServices.description: Well-regarded community library offering extensive programs and resources.
details: Located at 375 Kinderkamack Road
Children's programs, teen events, and adult book clubs
Free Wi-Fi, computer access, and digital resources
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
details: Youth sports leagues, swimming, and summer programs
Adult fitness and senior programs
Community events and holiday celebrations
Memorial Field and other park facilities

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Oradell enforces noise regulations to maintain the peace of its residential neighborhoods.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM weekdays
Saturday construction 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to daytime hours
Amplified music and loud gatherings subject to limits after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Properties must be maintained in good condition per borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns and landscaping must be maintained
Properties must be free of debris and unsightly conditions
Structures and fences must be kept in good repair
Sidewalks must be cleared of snow within 24 hours
Trees and shrubs must not obstruct sidewalks or sightlines

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Pet owners must follow licensing and leash requirements.
keyRules: Dogs must be licensed annually with the Borough Clerk
Dogs must be leashed when off owner's property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Barking dogs subject to complaints and fines
Cat licensing required

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking rules are enforced throughout the borough.
keyRules: No overnight parking on streets from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM
Vehicles must be moved during declared snow emergencies
No parking within 25 feet of intersections or fire hydrants
Downtown parking subject to posted time limits
Commuter permits required at the train station lot

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and maintained per borough ordinances.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of habitability required before tenant occupancy
Properties must meet code and safety requirements
Landlords responsible for ongoing maintenance
Short-term rentals subject to zoning restrictions

other.title: Reservoir Area Regulations
other.summary: The area surrounding the Oradell Reservoir is subject to special restrictions to protect the water supply.
keyRules: No swimming, boating, or fishing in the Oradell Reservoir
Reservoir perimeter walking paths are open to the public
No littering or dumping near the reservoir
Dogs must be leashed on reservoir trails
Ridgewood Water manages reservoir operations and access

schools.publicDistrict: Oradell Public Schools / River Dell Regional High School District

publicSchools.name: Oradell Public School
publicSchools.notes: Borough's elementary school known for strong academics and community involvement

publicSchools.name: River Dell Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Regional middle school located in Oradell serving Oradell and River Edge students

publicSchools.name: River Dell Regional High School
publicSchools.notes: Regional high school serving Oradell and River Edge; strong academics and athletics

privateSchools.name: Bergen Catholic High School
privateSchools.notes: All-boys Catholic college-prep school located in Oradell; nationally recognized athletics programs

whyMoveHere: Walkable downtown with local shops and restaurants
Scenic Oradell Reservoir and surrounding green space
Good schools in the River Dell Regional district
NJ Transit train station in the borough for NYC commuters
Classic, tree-lined Bergen County suburban neighborhoods

commuteToNYC.duration: 50-70 minutes to Hoboken, then PATH to NYC
commuteToNYC.notes: The Oradell station on the Pascack Valley Line provides rail service to Hoboken Terminal, where commuters connect to PATH trains for Midtown or Downtown Manhattan. NJ Transit bus routes also serve the borough. Route 4 and the George Washington Bridge are accessible for driving commuters.

costOfLiving.summary: Oradell is a mid-to-upper-range Bergen County community with home prices reflecting the walkable downtown, school quality, and reservoir proximity.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Above the state average for housing and property taxes

pros: Walkable downtown — rare for Bergen County suburbs
Train station in borough for NYC commuters
Scenic Oradell Reservoir provides green space and nature
Bergen Catholic adds to the educational options
Classic suburban character with strong property values
cons: Pascack Valley Line has limited off-peak train service
Property taxes are high relative to home values
Some streets experience traffic during school hours (Bergen Catholic)
Limited parking near the downtown and train station
Older housing stock may require renovation

restaurants.name: New Henley's Eatery
restaurants.description: Local restaurant offering contemporary American cuisine in a warm setting.

restaurants.name: Pizzaiola Café
restaurants.description: Neighborhood Italian café known for wood-fired pizza and homemade pasta.

restaurants.name: Seoul Garden
restaurants.description: Korean restaurant offering BBQ, bibimbap, and traditional Korean dishes.

restaurants.name: Glen Rock Inn (nearby)
restaurants.description: Classic American restaurant just across the border in River Edge, popular for steaks and comfort food.

parks.name: Oradell Reservoir
parks.description: Scenic reservoir surrounded by walking paths and natural green space, providing a peaceful setting for walks and nature observation.

parks.name: Memorial Field
parks.description: Borough sports complex with ball fields, track, and recreational facilities used by school and community sports leagues.

parks.name: Soldier Hill Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with playground, picnic areas, and open green space for families.

events.name: Oradell Day
events.description: Annual community celebration featuring food vendors, live music, games, rides, and family activities.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Festive tree lighting ceremony in the downtown area with holiday music and refreshments.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Community parade honoring veterans, featuring local schools, scouts, and civic organizations.

attractions.name: Oradell Reservoir
attractions.description: The borough's signature landmark — a scenic reservoir that serves as a regional water supply and provides a beautiful natural setting for walking and nature enjoyment.

attractions.name: Downtown Oradell
attractions.description: Walkable downtown district along Kinderkamack Road with local shops, restaurants, and the train station.

attractions.name: Bergen Catholic High School
attractions.description: Nationally recognized Catholic boys' school known for its championship athletic programs and notable alumni.

shoppingAreas: Nearby River Edge and Paramus shopping

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
